summary: I compared the build diff against the 13 business requirements from Define and traced each one to concrete evidence in the implementation. Default-to-system behavior (R1/R2) is handled by the pre-paint `_theme_init_script` reading `prefers-color-scheme` with a light fallback when `matchMedia` is unavailable; the toggle (R3) is present in the sidebar on every app page and in the auth layout; the Stimulus `theme_controller` switches themes in place, immediately and both directions (R4–R6), and keeps `aria-pressed` and the sun/moon icon in sync (R7); the choice persists in a one-year cookie that `ThemeHelper#dark_theme?` reads for server-side rendering (R8) and takes precedence over the OS setting (R9); the controller only follows OS changes when no stored preference exists (R10) and ignores them once a manual choice is made (R11); dark coverage is comprehensive through semantic CSS tokens redeclared under `.dark`, with the only remaining raw gray classes (in `_flash`) all carrying `dark:` variants (R12); and status/flash indicators retain dark variants while always showing the status word so color-encoded meaning is preserved (R13). Every requirement is satisfied, so I emitted a **pass** verdict with no findings to `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/requirements-conformance-critic/verdict.json` (noting that the suite couldn't be run here due to missing gems, though the build-phase test-critic already validated the tests).
